name: Chocolate Angel Food Layer Cake
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Family Circle, vol. 105 no. 3, 2/1/93
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Position oven rack in the bottom third of the oven. Line the bottoms of two 9-inch heart-shaped cake pans with waxed paper. Sift together 1/2 cup sugar, flour, cocoa powder, and salt onto a clean sheet of waxed paper.
2. Beat egg whites in a bowl at medium speed until foamy. Gradually add cream of tartar and vanilla, then beat in the remaining sugar, 1 tablespoon at a time, until soft peaks form.
3. Sift half of the flour mixture over the egg whites. Using a rubber spatula, fold in just until incorporated. Repeat with the remaining flour mixture. Divide batter evenly between prepared pans and smooth the tops.
4. Bake in the preheated oven at 350°F for 20 minutes or until the cake springs back when gently pressed with a fingertip and the sides of the cake begin to pull away from the pan. Invert the pans onto wire racks. Elevate the cakes by placing the corners of the racks over filled 1-pound cans. Let the cakes cool, upside down in the pans, on elevated racks for 1 hour.
5. Turn off heat source (e.g., oven) and let cakes cool completely before serving. Choose topping options: sandwiching layers as directed or cutting a heart from the center of one layer with a 1-1/2-inch border. Spread the other layer with some Cherry-Yogurt Filling, top with the cutout layer, and fill the hollow with remaining cherries. Refrigerate.
NOTES: See recipe for Cherry-Yogurt Filling
